channel.title: Entertainment News | CableTV.com
channel.description: Super fans, rejoice! Our entertainment news keeps you passionate about sports, TV, and streaming.

image.title: Industry News: TV, Streaming & Internet Updates | CableTV.com

item.title: It’s Not Just ‘Trash TV’—Here’s Why You’re Addicted to the Drama
item.dc:creator: Sage Singleton
item.description:
Is reality TV a guilty pleasure? We surveyed fans for the real story. See our exclusive 2026 data on viewing habits and fan sentiment.

The post It’s Not Just ‘Trash TV’—Here’s Why You’re Addicted to the Drama appeared first on CableTV.com.
item.content:encoded:
They say beauty is in the eye of the beholder—and the same seems to apply to television. One viewer’s trash TV is another’s favorite guilty pleasure. In fact, according to recent survey data from self-proclaimed reality TV fans, 75% still describe the genre as a “guilty pleasure.”

75% of Americans still describe reality TV as a guilty pleasure.

Love it or love to hate it, the numbers are hard to ignore. Hundreds of millions of viewers stream buzzy hits like “Love Is Blind,” while long-running staples like “The Bachelor” and “The Bachelorette” still pull in roughly three to five million viewers per episode.

And fans are tuning in in all kinds of ways. Nearly half of respondents (47%) say they watch reality TV solo, while 32% turn it into a watch-party event. Another 18% say reality shows are their go-to entertainment on the treadmill or doing chores.

However you watch it, one thing is clear: With a steady stream of new shows, viral moments and breakout personalities, reality TV isn’t going anywhere anytime soon.

Thou shalt sign up for the drama: How reality TV is driving streaming signups

Case in point: The highly anticipated new season of “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” launched on Hulu on March 12 and promises another round of #MomTok drama.

We wanted to know just how much the drama draws viewers in — and the results suggest it’s a major driver of new signups.

Nearly two-thirds of respondents (64%) say they’ve already signed up for or plan to sign up for Hulu specifically to watch the new season of “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives,” along with the upcoming season of “The Bachelorette” featuring #MomTok queen Taylor Frankie Paul.

Looking for the hottest entertainment?

Subscribe to CableTV.com's email newsletter for the latest TV event viewing guides, entertaining takes, and money-saving promotions.

By signing up, you agree to our Privacy Policy & Terms and Conditions.

That’s good news for streaming platforms leaning into buzzy reality franchises—viewers are clearly willing to subscribe if it means getting the latest tea.

Even among those who aren’t planning to tune in to “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives,” the behavior holds. Six in 10 respondents say they’ve signed up for a new streaming service solely to watch a favorite reality show, and 82% say they typically keep the subscription even after the season ends.

It turns out viewers aren’t just watching for the drama, they’re also picking favorites. Half of the respondents say they enjoy watching “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives,” and when asked to rank the cast, Jen claimed the top spot, followed by Taylor Frankie Paul and Layla. The rest of the fan-favorite lineup includes Whitney, Mikayla, Miranda, Mayci, Jessi, and Demi.

Favorite “Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” cast members, ranked

  1. Jen
  2. Taylor
  3. Layla
  4. Whitney
  5. Mikayla
  6. Miranda
  7. Mayci
  8. Jessi
  9. Demi

Some of that popularity is already extending beyond the Hulu series. Several cast members have appeared in other entertainment projects, including Jen and Whitney’s appearances on “Dancing With the Stars” last season, Whitney’s run on Broadway, and Taylor Frankie Paul’s upcoming turn as the lead on the next season of “The Bachelorette,” airing March 22, 2026.

With a #MomTok favorite now stepping into one of reality TV’s most recognizable franchises, we also wanted to see how audiences are watching “The Bachelorette” and what keeps them tuning in season after season.

Roses, reality, and ratings

Now entering its 30th season, “The Bachelor” and “The Bachelorette” franchise remains a longtime fan favorite. While Taylor Frankie Paul’s appearance is drawing in some new viewers (15%), many fans say they’d be tuning in regardless—one in four respondents plan to watch the upcoming season no matter who’s handing out the roses.

Pro tip: For those who’ve been living under a rock, you can stream “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” and The Bachelor universe on Hulu.

The franchise’s staying power is clear in the longevity of its audience. The average fan says they’ve been invested in the series for at least five years, suggesting viewers keep coming back season after season to see how the love stories unfold.

Social media is also playing a growing role in drawing people in. Thirty percent of respondents say they’ve been influenced to watch the show after seeing clips online, a sign that viral moments and trending content are shaping viewing habits. Meanwhile, 26% say they simply enjoy dating reality shows in general, making “The Bachelorette” just the latest stop in their rotation of romantic reality TV.

After the final rose: The reality shows fans love the most

So what happens after the final rose has been accepted? Our survey looked beyond a single franchise to see which reality shows viewers keep coming back to. About 30% of viewers say they tune in to reality TV for the tea, while 29% say they watch because they enjoy the cast and storylines.

And while dating shows dominate much of the reality TV conversation, the long-running competition series “Survivor,” now in its 50th season, actually ranks as viewers’ top favorite overall, with the Bachelor franchise close behind.

Top 5 favorite reality TV shows and franchises in 2026

  1. “Survivor”
  2. “The Bachelor”
  3. “The Bachelorette”
  4. Love Island
  5. The Real Housewives

How viewers are watching the drama

So how are fans watching all this reality TV content? Streaming remains the dominant way to binge-watch, with 59% of respondents saying they tune in through streaming platforms.

Still, traditional TV isn’t out of the picture just yet. About 29% of viewers say they still watch episodes live, suggesting reality TV continues to be one of the few genres keeping audiences tuned into scheduled programming.

How Americans watch reality TV

  • 59% streaming full episodes on a streaming platform
  • 29% watch live on TV when they air
  • 12% watch recorded episodes or social media clips

The rest of us are catching up in other ways—from recorded episodes to social media recaps that let fans stay in the loop without watching every minute. In an era of viral clips and trending moments, sometimes the drama travels just as far on TikTok or Instagram as it does on screen.

Whether it’s tuning in for the tea, following a favorite cast member, or signing up for a new streaming service just to keep up with the drama, one thing is clear: reality TV continues to hold viewers’ attention.

From longtime franchises like “The Bachelor” and “Survivor” to buzzy newcomers like “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives,” audiences are still eager to watch the drama unfold, however and wherever it streams next.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts—we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • The 2026 Oscars on Hulu
  • Why You Still Subscribe to Cable in 2026
  • Netflix Is Out, Paramount Wants Warner Bros.

The post It’s Not Just ‘Trash TV’—Here’s Why You’re Addicted to the Drama appeared first on CableTV.com.

item.title: Why Ranking Paul Thomas Anderson Movies Is Impossible (But We Tried Anyway)
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com Staff
item.description:
Paul Thomas Anderson has never made a bad movie. CableTV.com loves PTA and decided to rank all 10 of his films. Our list may surprise you.

The post Why Ranking Paul Thomas Anderson Movies Is Impossible (But We Tried Anyway) appeared first on CableTV.com.
item.content:encoded:
✦ Edited and fact-checked by Alex Vejar, our Staff Writer with 10 years of experience covering entertainment and sports.

Every Paul Thomas Anderson movie ranked from worst to best

With “One Battle After Another” being the current frontrunner for multiple Academy Awards, we thought it would be fun to take a look at PTA’s entire filmography. Whether you love sprawling ’70s ensembles or meticulous period dramas, there’s no denying that he’s one of the most dynamic filmmakers in modern cinema.

We analyzed the critical consensus from Rotten Tomatoes, “The Hollywood Reporter,” IndieWire, and “Variety,” then inserted our own opinions to bring you the definitive ranking of his filmography. From his high-energy beginnings to his most recent masterpieces—yes, there are multiple—we’re diving deep into what makes a PTA film so special.

If you’re looking for more cinematic greatness, be sure to check out our guides on the best Leonardo DiCaprio movies or see which films made the cut in our 2026 Oscar predictions.

10. “Inherent Vice” (2014)

This is easily the most polarizing film in Anderson’s career. A “stoner noir” adaptation of the Thomas Pynchon novel, “Inherent Vice” is intentionally confusing, hazy, and thick with 1970s atmosphere. While some critics find it a misunderstood masterpiece of comedic timing and subversion, others find it too jumbled and inaccessible to rank. PTA has never really made a bad movie, but this is one with the least rewatchabilty. Still, the performances from Joaquin Phoenix and Josh Brolin make it worth at least one viewing.

9. “Hard Eight” (1996)

While it’s universally cited as his “weakest” effort, “Hard Eight” was an early sign of directing promise from PTA. This grounded, cool neo-noir follows a veteran gambler who takes a young down-on-his-luck man under his wing in Reno, and features great performances from Philip Baker Hall and John C. Reilly. The chaos that ensues in the film feels reminiscent of “Boogie Nights” and “Magnoia.”

8. “Licorice Pizza” (2021)

Highly rated by modern critics, “Licorice Pizza” was occasionally docked for its controversial age-gap romance (who cares, if you ask us) between Alana Haim and Cooper Hoffman’s characters. The debut performances of Haim and Hoffman are what most shine in this film, followed by the incredible car-in-reverse set piece. This movie is still a charming gem, but doesn’t stand a chance against PTA’s heavy hitters.

7. “Magnolia” (1999)

The ultimate love-it-or-hate-it epic, “Magnolia” is a sprawling, emotional, and at times confusing three-hour ensemble piece about coincidence, regret, and raining frogs. It has some of the highest highs in his entire filmography—including what many consider to be Tom Cruise’s best performance as a hyper-masculine motivational speaker. It’s bold, messy, and PTA’s most ambitious film that doesn’t totally work, but holds a special place in our hearts.

6. “The Master” (2012)

This is the most artsy of PTA’s efforts and often cited as his most intellectual and technically perfect film. “The Master” explores a brooding, enigmatic power struggle between a drifter and a charismatic cult leader, and features towering performances by Joaquin Phoenix and Philip Seymour Hoffman. It can be a challenging watch for casual viewers, but its depth leaves plenty to chew on for cinephiles who love and return to it.

5. “Punch-Drunk Love” (2002)

This film is a perennial critical darling for its brilliant subversion of the Adam Sandler persona. An anxious, candy-colored romantic comedy, “Punch-Drunk Love” feels like a panic attack turned into a love story. Sandler is electric and sympathetic, and Hoffman steals the few scenes he’s in. This is a masterpiece on PTA’s terms—smaller than his three-hour epics, but packs just as much punch.

4. “One Battle After Another” (2025)

Putting a masterpiece outside the top three feels criminal, but that doesn’t mean this film isn’t pristine. “One Battle After Another,” starring Leonardo DiCaprio, is a politically charged, action-tinged epic hailed as his most urgent work, illuminating themes centered around white supremacy, aging, father-daughter relationships, and the human side of political activism. It synthesizes all the films that came before it for what feels PTA’s most complete work.

3. “Phantom Thread” (2017)

This late-career masterpiece has climbed the rankings every year since its release. A gothic romance about a fastidious dressmaker and his stubborn muse, “Phantom Thread” is arguably Anderson’s most flawless film from a craft perspective. The costumes in this film are immaculate and worth the film’s runtime alone. It’s also, oddly, perhaps his most romantic film, and it’s hilarious in unexpected ways. The best part? It’s sneakily more about the female lead character.

2. “Boogie Nights” (1997)

This film made him PTA a superstar. “Boogie Nights” is a high-octane, Scorsese-esque journey depicting the rise and fall of porn star Dirk Diggler, played by Mark Wahlberg, in the late ’70s and early ’80s. It’s his most energetic film, packed with unforgettable characters and iconic needle drops. While it doesn’t top the list here, it’s No. 1 for many, many people.

1. “There Will Be Blood” (2007)

“There Will Be Blood” is the undisputed heavyweight champion of Paul Thomas Anderson movies. It’s a staggeringly dark epic about oil, religion, opportunism, and the brutal side of the American Dream. It is the only film that ranks No. 1 on nearly every list, from Rotten Tomatoes to Variety, and our absolute favorite to watch over and over again. Between Daniel Day-Lewis’s iconic performance as Daniel Plainview, the haunting score, and the inimitable Paul Dano, it is widely considered one of the greatest films of the 21st century.

Paul Thomas Anderson movies FAQ

What was Paul Thomas Anderson's first movie?

Paul Thomas Anderson's directorial debut was "Hard Eight" (1996), originally titled "Sydney." It stars Philip Baker Hall, John C. Reilly, and Gwyneth Paltrow.

How many Oscars has Paul Thomas Anderson won?

Despite being nominated for over 10 Academy Awards across categories like Best Director, Best Picture, and Best Screenplay, Paul Thomas Anderson has never won an Oscar. But his films have won several awards for his cast and crew, including Best Actor for Daniel Day-Lewis.

Where can I stream Paul Thomas Anderson movies?

Most of his films are available across various platforms like Paramount+, HBO Max, and Netflix. For a complete breakdown of which service carries your favorite director, check out our streaming service reviews.

What other projects has Paul Thomas Anderson been part of?

Aside from feature films, Paul Thomas Anderson is known for directing many music videos with artists such as Haim, Radiohead, and Fiona Apple. He's also directed more than a dozen short films and the documentary called "Junun" (2015).

What to binge next

  • How to Watch the Oscars
  • Oscars Nominees on Streaming
  • Best Leonardo DiCaprio Movies

The post Why Ranking Paul Thomas Anderson Movies Is Impossible (But We Tried Anyway) appeared first on CableTV.com.

item.title: Move Over, ‘The Office’—There’s a New King of Workplace Cringe
item.dc:creator: Ian McLaren
item.description:
Watch the chaos! Get the full streaming schedule for "Jury Duty Presents: Company Retreat." See how to watch Season 2 on Prime Video starting March 20.

The post Move Over, ‘The Office’—There’s a New King of Workplace Cringe appeared first on CableTV.com.
item.content:encoded:
You’re ordered to stream “Jury Duty Presents: Company Retreat” this month

The first season of the mockumentary series “Jury Duty” was a surprise cultural phenomenon, earning critical acclaim and a devoted fanbase for its heartwarming, high-stakes prank on the lovable Ronald Gladden.

It even secured a Peabody Award and an Emmy nomination for Outstanding Comedy Series and gave us all a renewed appreciation for James Marden (once, like Ronald, we remembered he stars in the “Sonic the Hedgehog” movie).

And after a long wait for more unscripted chaos, the next chapter is finally here.

Instead of a courtroom, this season takes us to the corporate world. Here is everything you need to know about the release date, the new cast, and how to watch.

What is “Jury Duty Presents: Company Retreat” about?

The new season follows Anthony, a real-life temp worker who believes he has been hired to help organize a corporate “away day” for a company called Rockin’ Grandma’s Hot Sauce.

Just like the first season, Anthony is the only person who isn’t in on the joke. Everyone around him is an actor, and the entire retreat is staged.

Over the course of eight episodes, Anthony will navigate the final retreat for the company’s retiring CEO, Doug, while a fake private-equity team attempts a hostile takeover.

“Jury Duty Presents: Company Retreat” release date

“Jury Duty Presents: Company Retreat” (technically Season 2 of “Jury Duty”) premieres on Friday, March 20, 2026. Unlike the previous season, which was on Freevee, this installment is a Prime Video exclusive, requiring a subscription to Amazon’s streaming service.

“Jury Duty Presents: Company Retreat” release schedule

The series follows a staggered release format to keep the mystery alive. Here’s the streaming release calendar for 2026:

  • March 20, 2026: Series premiere (episodes 1–3)
  • March 27, 2026: Mid-season drop (episodes 4–5)
  • April 3, 2026: The grand finale (episodes 6–8)

How to watch “Jury Duty Presents: Company Retreat”

To access “Company Retreat” and follow the journey of the unsuspecting hero, use the following methods:

  • Primary platform: Access the series through Amazon Prime Video. Unlike the first season, this is an exclusive “Prime Original” release.
  • Device compatibility: The series is available on the Prime Video app for Smart TVs, Fire TV, Roku, Apple TV, PlayStation/Xbox, and mobile devices.
  • Web access: Stream directly via a web browser at amazon.com/primevideo.
  • Account requirements: You will need an active Amazon Prime membership or a standalone Prime Video subscription to watch.
  • Download for offline viewing: Mobile users can download episodes to watch during their own commutes or travels.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts—we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • Disney Just Renewed 4 Shows, But Cancelled a Classic
  • New on Prime Video March 2026
  • What To Watch on Prime Video

The post Move Over, ‘The Office’—There’s a New King of Workplace Cringe appeared first on CableTV.com.

item.title: This Fantasy Series Is Finally Getting an Anime, and You Can Watch It Early
item.dc:creator: Olivia Bono
item.description:
When does the 'Witch Hat Atelier' anime come to streaming? Let's talk premiere date, English trailer, and more details for anime super-fans!

The post This Fantasy Series Is Finally Getting an Anime, and You Can Watch It Early appeared first on CableTV.com.
item.content:encoded:
A world of witches and magic

If you’re a fan of fantasy manga, you might be familiar with “Witch Hat Atelier.” It’s a very popular story about an apprentice witch in a world where rune-based magic is a carefully guarded secret, practiced only by witches who wear distinctive pointy hats.

The original manga started publication in 2016, and the anime adaptation was actually supposed to come out in 2025. However, production delays pushed that date to 2026, and now the long-anticipated “Witch Hat Atelier” adaptation is finally coming out this April.

Let’s talk about the “Witch Hat Atelier” premiere date—and how you might be able to watch it early (if you’re near Seattle).

When is the “Witch Hat Atelier” premiere date?

“Witch Hat Atelier” will premiere on Crunchyroll on April 6. The premiere date was revealed in a recent trailer, which you can watch below. The trailer is subtitled in English, but it is not yet clear whether there will be an English dub.

How to watch “Witch Hat Atelier” early

Anime fans attending Sakura-Con will be able to catch the first two episodes early on April 4 at 10:30 a.m. PT in the Seattle Convention Center’s Ballroom 2.

Crunchyroll will also be screening several other anime previews in Ballroom 2 as part of its Crunchyroll Presents series. Other titles include “Classroom of the Elite,” “Dr. STONE SCIENCE FUTURE,” and “LIAR GAME.”

But we East Coasters will have to wait for the anime season to officially start. Wondering when each title will premiere on streaming? Check back later in the month for our full Anime Simulcast Schedule, featuring premieres from Crunchyroll, Netflix, Hulu, and more.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts—we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • Upcoming Anime Simulcasts
  • New Yoshi Voice Actor Revealed
  • Watch ‘One Piece’ Season 2

The post This Fantasy Series Is Finally Getting an Anime, and You Can Watch It Early appeared first on CableTV.com.

item.title: 11 Wagner Moura Movies and TV Shows That Rate at Least a 7
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com Staff
item.description:
From "Narcos" to his 2026 Oscar nod for "The Secret Agent," see the 11 best Wagner Moura movies and shows that have a 7+ rating. Click for the full list!

The post 11 Wagner Moura Movies and TV Shows That Rate at Least a 7 appeared first on CableTV.com.
item.content:encoded:
✦ Edited and fact-checked by Randy Harward, our Entertainment Editor with 25 years covering entertainment and TV.

11 best Wagner Moura movies and TV shows according to IMDb

Wagner Moura is currently at the apex of his career, successfully transitioning from a Brazilian powerhouse to a globally celebrated leading man. While many of us first met him during his star-making turn as Pablo Escobar in “Narcos,” he has spent the last two decades building a filmography that features 59 acting credits and a growing list of directorial and producer roles.

By 2026, he has become a primary face of prestige cinema. We’ve watched him level up with complex leading turns that move far beyond the drug dealer stereotypes he once feared, culminating in a 2026 Oscar nomination for Best Actor. Whether he is voicing a terrifying villain or portraying a bedraggled journalist, his linguistic and physical dedication to his craft is pure gold.

11. “Dope Thief” (2025) | 7.0

In this gritty Apple TV+ crime drama, Moura stars alongside Brian Tyree Henry as a pair of low-rent Philly thieves who bite off more than they can chew. When their scheme to rob drug dealers by posing as DEA agents goes sideways, they find themselves hunted by a global narcotics empire. Executive produced by Ridley Scott, the series highlights Moura’s incredible chemistry with his co-stars and his ability to make small-time characters feel unbelievably big-time.

10. “Civil War” (2024) | 7.0

In this intense Alex Garland vision, we follow a team of military-embedded journalists as they race against time to reach Washington, D.C. Moura delivers a standout performance as Joel, a bedraggled and adrenaline-fueled journalist navigating a fractured landscape. The film serves as a chilling exploration of political polarization and the high cost of documenting the truth in a war zone.

9. “The Man from the Future” (2011) | 7.0

We love seeing Moura’s range, and this sci-fi rom-com proves he can handle whimsical high-concept plots just as well as gritty dramas. He plays Zero, a brilliant scientist who accidentally travels back 20 years to the night he was publicly humiliated by the love of his life. It is a charming romp that showcases his comedic timing and a softer side we don’t always see in his intense political roles.

8. “The Secret Agent” (2025) | 7.3

This political thriller served as a homecoming for Moura, addressing the heavy themes of Brazil’s military dictatorship. Playing a man caught in a web of surveillance, Moura’s performance was so moving it earned him Best Actor at the 2025 Cannes Film Festival and a 2026 Golden Globe. The film has dominated the awards circuit as Brazil’s official international feature submission, solidifying his status as a world-class leading man.

7. “Shining Girls” (2022) | 7.4

In this mind-bending Apple TV+ series, Moura plays Dan Velasquez, a veteran journalist who teams up with a trauma survivor to hunt down a time-traveling serial killer. We found his chemistry with Elisabeth Moss to be electric, providing a grounded, human heart to a supernatural mystery. His portrayal of a father and reporter battling his own demons adds a layer of vulnerability that makes the investigation feel deeply personal.

6. “Carandiru” (2003) | 7.6

Long before he was a global household name, Moura appeared in this harrowing drama about the 1992 massacre at the Carandiru Penitentiary. The story is told through the eyes of a doctor treating inmates, revealing the overcrowded and inhumane conditions of the prison system. Moura plays Zico, part of a sprawling ensemble cast that captures the tragic and vibrant lives of the men behind bars.

5. “Puss in Boots: The Last Wish” (2022) | 7.8

We have to talk about the whistle. Moura provided the voice for the Wolf (also known as Death), creating one of the most terrifying and celebrated animated villains in recent memory. His chilling, predatory performance turned a kids’ movie into a profound meditation on mortality. It is a testament to his talent that he can steal an entire film using nothing but his voice and a hauntingly melodic whistle.

4. “Elite Squad” (2007) | 8.0

This was the breakout moment that changed everything. As Capitão Nascimento, the leader of an elite police squad in Rio de Janeiro, Moura portrays the brutal reality of urban warfare and systemic corruption. The film won the Golden Bear at the Berlin International Film Festival and became a cultural phenomenon in Brazil. His intense, uncompromising performance turned Nascimento into an anti-hero icon and put Moura on the international map.

3. “Elite Squad: The Enemy Within” (2010) | 8.0

Sequels rarely outshine the original, but this one comes incredibly close by expanding the scope from the streets to the halls of political power. Now working in a high-level government position, Nascimento realizes that the real enemy is the corrupt politicians benefiting from the chaos. It is a smarter, more cynical, and deeply impactful follow-up that remains one of the highest-rated Brazilian films of all time.

2. “Narcos” (2015–2016) | 8.7

The role that defined his early international career required Moura to learn Spanish from scratch and gain significant weight to play the infamous Pablo Escobar. While some native speakers debated his accent, we were captivated by his pure gold acting and his ability to make a monster feel human. His performance earned him his first Golden Globe nomination in 2016 and remains the gold standard for portraying the Medellín cartel leader.

1. “Narcos: Mexico” (2018–2021) | 8.3

Though he only appears briefly to reprise his role as Escobar, the impact of his character looms large over the entire series. This expansion of the “Narcos” universe explores the rise of the Guadalajara Cartel in the 1980s, shifting the focus to the origins of the Mexican drug war. The series maintains the high-octane tension and prestige production value that Moura helped establish, earning its spot at the top of our list.

Wagner Moura movies and TV shows FAQ

What are the best Wagner Moura movies and TV shows?

Based on IMDb ratings and critical acclaim, Wagner Moura's best works include "Narcos" (8.7), "Narcos: Mexico" (8.3), "Elite Squad" (8.0), and the award-winning 2025 film "The Secret Agent" (7.3).

Has Wagner Moura won an Oscar?

As of early 2026, Wagner Moura is a first-time Oscar nominee for Best Actor for his role in "The Secret Agent." He has previously won Best Actor at the Cannes Film Festival and a Golden Globe for the same performance.

Did Wagner Moura really learn Spanish for Narcos?

Yes. Despite being a native Portuguese speaker from Brazil, Moura moved to Medellín, Colombia, to immerse himself in the language and culture, learning Spanish from scratch to play Pablo Escobar.

What to binge next

  • How To Watch the 2026 Oscars
  • How To Stream Every Best Picture Nominee
  • How Many Timothee Chalamet Movies Can You Watch Before the Oscars?

The post 11 Wagner Moura Movies and TV Shows That Rate at Least a 7 appeared first on CableTV.com.

item.title: A Busted March Madness Bracket Can Still Win You $500—Here’s How
item.dc:creator: Alex Vejar
item.description:
CableTV.com is giving away $500 and gift cards during March Madness to whoever has the most accurate NCAA Tournament bracket.

The post A Busted March Madness Bracket Can Still Win You $500—Here’s How appeared first on CableTV.com.
item.content:encoded:
CableTV.com wants you to join its first-ever March Madness bracket challenge

The NCAA Tournament is one of the marquee events in all of sports and proves every year that, truly, anything can happen. It’s also become synonymous with fans filling out their own brackets for fun, for money, and to prove that they know ball.

This year, CableTV.com is giving college basketball fans a unique opportunity to not only secure bragging rights among friends but also win a nice chunk of change in the process.

By joining our official ESPN Bracket Challenge Group and filling out a bracket, fans have a chance of winning a grand prize of $500. The bracket with the most points at the end of the NCAA Tournament wins.

“CableTV.com is known for entertainment, sports, and legendary giveaways,” said Craig Stirland, the digital public relations specialist at CableTV.com. “Our team loves the tournament, so we wanted to give our followers a chance to have fun and compete for a nice payday.”

In addition to the cash prize, CableTV.com is giving fans multiple opportunities via its social media accounts to win gift cards for Homefield merchandise.

How to enter the CableTV.com Bracket Challenge

You can enter CableTV.com’s March Madness bracket challenge by following these simple steps below. You don’t need to create a bracket before joining the group. Just remember to make one before the March 19 deadline.

  1. Visit ESPN’s Men’s Tournament Challenge page.
  2. Go to the Groups tab, and search for CableTV.com.
  3. Click Join Group, and enter the password cabletv500.
  4. Finalize your bracket on March 19, before first-round games begin.

The contest is open to all legal residents of the United States who are 18 years of age or older at the time they enter. In the event of a tie, the prize money will be divided equally among the winners.

Who we are

CableTV.com is a website that helps people find the best ways to watch their favorite TV, movies, and sports content on cable, satellite, or streaming. We use the expertise of in-house and freelance experts to give recommendations on TV and streaming plans. We also use proprietary data to report on which services customers like the most and why.

To learn more about how we do our work, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for sports fans

  • MLB.TV Moves to ESPN for Three Years: What Does the Shakeup Mean for Fans?
  • Fubo Blackout Explained: NBC, USA Network, and RSNs Go Dark in Contract Dispute
  • NBC Sports Network Is Back: What the Relaunch Really Means for Sports Fans

The post A Busted March Madness Bracket Can Still Win You $500—Here’s How appeared first on CableTV.com.

item.title: Hannah Montana Makes a Surprising Return With New Disney+ Content
item.dc:creator: Olivia Bono
item.description:
Find the official Disney+ premiere date for the 'Hannah Montana 20th Anniversary Special' and watch the newly released trailer right here.

The post Hannah Montana Makes a Surprising Return With New Disney+ Content appeared first on CableTV.com.
item.content:encoded:
A 20th Hannah-versary

This week, Disney revealed a new teaser for the upcoming “Hannah Montana” 20th anniversary special. “Already getting emotional,” says former Disney Channel star Miley Cyrus, back in her iconic Hannah Montana wig.

I knew that Miley was returning to Disney to reminisce about the show that jump-started her career, but it hadn’t really dawned on me that it had been 20 years since the first episode. “Hannah Montana” itself is older than Miley was (18) when the show ended.

If you’re like me and can remember watching the show’s premiere all those years ago on Disney Channel, you might want to check out this new special. Keep reading for more info on how you can watch the “Hannah Montana 20th Anniversary Special.”

When does the “Hannah Montana” special come to Disney+?

Can’t wait to bask in Hannah Montana lore and nostalgia? The “Hannah Montana 20th Anniversary Special” will air on Disney+ on March 24.

Not a Disney+ subscriber? We got you. Read our comprehensive Disney+ review to find a plan that works best for your household.

Get ready for an in-depth, behind-the-scenes look at the Disney Channel classic, including an interview with Miley Cyrus filmed in front of a live studio audience (sitcom-style).

Who we are

Our editorial staff here at CableTV.com are just like you—we get excited about our favorite shows and movies from our childhoods. That’s why we’re here, in this age of streaming services, to keep you informed about all of the reboots, reunions, and re-imaginings that Hollywood dreams up next. For more on what we do here, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • “Super Mario Galaxy Movie” Announces New Cast Members
  • “Zootopia 2” is Coming to Streaming
  • How to Stream Cartoon Network Classics for Free

The post Hannah Montana Makes a Surprising Return With New Disney+ Content appeared first on CableTV.com.

item.title: How Many Timothée Chalamet Movies Can You Watch Before the 2026 Oscars?
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com Staff
item.description:
From his 2026 Oscar-nominated role in "Marty Supreme" to "Dune" and "Call Me by Your Name," discover the best Timothée Chalamet movies to stream in March 2026.

The post How Many Timothée Chalamet Movies Can You Watch Before the 2026 Oscars? appeared first on CableTV.com.
item.content:encoded:
✦ Edited and fact-checked by Randy Harward, our Entertainment Editor with 25 years experience covering entertainment and TV.

Every Timothée Chalamet movie at a glance

Timothée Chalamet is the definitive leading man of 2026. As we head into the 98th Academy Awards, we’re revisiting our TikTok video to celebrate Chalamet officially entering his “prestige powerhouse” era. At just 30 years old, he is the youngest male actor since Marlon Brando to earn three Best Actor Oscar nominations—a historical trifecta that includes “Call Me by Your Name,” “A Complete Unknown,” and his latest A24 hit, “Marty Supreme.”

Whether you are a longtime “Chalamaniac” or just catching up on his record-breaking streak of eight Best Picture-nominated films, we have rounded up every single feature film performance you need to see. From his transformative turn as Bob Dylan to the desert sands of Arrakis, here is the complete guide to his filmography leading to his 2026 Oscar nomination.

26. “Dune: Part Three” (2026)

Currently in post-production with a scheduled release for Dec. 18, 2026, this film adapts “Dune Messiah,” Frank Herbert’s sequel to the original “Dune.” Production wrapped in Nov. 2025 and features a significant 12-year narrative jump for Chalamet’s Paul Atreides. We are expecting a “generational clash” at the box office as it opens against “Avengers: Doomsday.”

25. “Marty Supreme” (2025)

This is the role that has everyone talking for the 2026 Oscar nomination. In this Josh Safdie-directed flick, Chalamet delivers his most physically demanding performance yet as a table tennis hustler. “Marty Supreme” has officially become the highest-grossing North American release in A24’s history. If you missed the theatrical run, “Marty Supreme” streaming began on digital VOD (Prime Video, Apple TV) on Feb. 10, 2026, and is expected to arrive on HBO Max in April 2026.

24. “A Complete Unknown” (2024)

Chalamet completely disappeared into the role of a young Bob Dylan in this James Mangold biopic. His second Best Actor nomination came from this transformative performance, where he notably performed his own vocals to capture the folk legend’s 1960s transition. The film follows Dylan’s arrival in New York City with nothing but a few dollars and a guitar.

23. “Dune: Part Two” (2024)

In this massive Best Picture nominee, we see Paul Atreides evolve from a vengeful prince into a messianic leader of the Fremen. Chalamet anchors the blockbuster with a brooding, powerful presence as he navigates the complex politics and giant sandworms of Arrakis. This sequel proved that Chalamet could single-handedly anchor a global franchise.

22. “Wonka” (2023)

Chalamet took on the whimsical mantle of a young Willy Wonka. This musical origin story allowed him to show off his singing and dancing chops in a vibrant, family-friendly spectacle. The story focuses on Wonka’s early days as an eccentric chocolatier-in-the-making.

21. “Bones and All” (2022)

Reunited with director Luca Guadagnino, Chalamet stars as Lee, a disenfranchised drifter in this haunting cannibal romance road movie. This project also marked a professional milestone as one of the first major films where Chalamet served as a producer. It is a gritty, surprisingly beautiful look at marginalized people trying to find love.

20. “A Man Named Scott” (2021)

In this documentary feature focusing on the life and career of Kid Cudi, Chalamet appears as himself. The film explores the creative process and the impact of the influential musician’s work on his fans and peers. Chalamet’s inclusion highlights his deep-rooted connections within the music industry and his personal fandom.

19. “Don’t Look Up” (2021)

In this star-studded Adam McKay satire, Chalamet plays Yule, a skater-punk who finds a connection with Jennifer Lawrence’s character as the world faces an impending comet strike. Even in a smaller supporting role, he managed to steal scenes from industry titans. The film earned a Best Picture nomination, adding to Chalamet’s growing list of Academy favorites.

18. “Dune” (2021)

The film that started the spice-fueled phenomenon introduced us to Paul Atreides as a young man plagued by visions of a dark future. Chalamet’s performance captures the weight of destiny as his house is betrayed and he is forced into the harsh desert of Arrakis.

17. “The French Dispatch” (2021)

Working with Wes Anderson, Chalamet plays Zeffirelli, a student revolutionary in the fictional French city of Ennui-sur-Blasé. His segment, “Revisions to a Manifesto,” showcases his comedic timing and fits perfectly within Anderson’s highly stylized aesthetic.

16. “Little Women” (2019)

Playing the boy-next-door Laurie, Chalamet provided the perfect foil to Saoirse Ronan’s Jo March in Greta Gerwig’s acclaimed adaptation. His portrayal of the wealthy, somewhat aimless neighbor was both charming and heartbreaking, especially during the film’s famous rejection scene.

15. “The King” (2019)

Chalamet takes on the mantle of Hal, the wayward prince who must become King Henry V, in this gritty Netflix original. The film follows his transition from a life of debauchery to the grim realities of medieval warfare and political intrigue.

14. “A Rainy Day in New York” (2019)

In this Woody Allen film, Chalamet plays Gatsby Welles, a college student whose weekend trip to Manhattan with his girlfriend becomes a series of mishaps. The film features a vintage New York charm and explores themes of young love and the search for identity.

More from Oscar-nominated Actors

  • Top Wagner Moura Movies and TV Shows
  • Elle Fanning Movies and TV Shows
  • Ryan Coogler Movies to Watch Before the Oscars

13. “Beautiful Boy” (2018)

Chalamet delivers a gut-wrenching performance as Nic Sheff, a young man struggling with crystal meth addiction. Based on the memoirs of David and Nic Sheff, the film captures the devastating cycle of relapse and the toll it takes on a father-son relationship.

12. “Call Me by Your Name” (2017)

The film that launched him into superstardom. As Elio Perlman, Chalamet delivered a raw, vulnerable performance that earned him his first Best Actor Oscar nomination at age 22. Set in 1980s Italy, the film captures the intensity of first love and heartbreak with breathtaking beauty.

11. “Lady Bird” (2017)

In another collaboration with Greta Gerwig, Chalamet plays Kyle Scheible, the ultimate pretentious teenage boyfriend. While he isn’t the hero of this story, his “douchey” charm was a standout element of this coming-of-age classic.

10. “Hot Summer Nights” (2017)

Set in Cape Cod in the early 1990s, Chalamet plays Daniel, an awkward teenager who gets in over his head dealing drugs. The film balances a nostalgic summer vibe with a spiraling crime narrative.

9. “Hostiles” (2017)

In this brutal Western, Chalamet has a supporting role as Pvt. Philippe De Jardin, a young French soldier. He joins a legendary Army captain on a dangerous journey to escort a dying Cheyenne chief back to his tribal lands.

8. “Miss Stevens” (2016)

Chalamet plays Billy, a troubled and talented student attending a drama competition in this heartfelt indie drama. His performance, particularly a powerful monologue from “Death of a Salesman,” was an early indicator of his massive talent.

7. “The Adderall Diaries” (2015)

Playing the teenage version of Stephen, Chalamet appears in flashbacks that haunt the protagonist’s present. The story deals with memory, truth, and a writer’s obsession with a high-profile murder trial.

6. “Love the Coopers” (2015)

In this ensemble holiday comedy, Chalamet plays Charlie, a teenager navigating family dysfunction during Christmas. He is part of four generations of the Cooper family who come together for an annual celebration that goes spectacularly off the rails.

5. “One & Two” (2015)

This indie thriller features Chalamet as Zac, one of two siblings who discover they possess supernatural abilities to teleport. Growing up in a strict, isolated household, they must use their powers to escape their father’s control.

4. “Interstellar” (2014)

In his breakout blockbuster role, Chalamet plays the younger version of Tom, son of Matthew McConaughey’s Cooper. While much of the film takes place in deep space, Chalamet’s performance provides the emotional anchor for the life Cooper is leaving behind on Earth.

3. “Men, Women & Children” (2014)

Chalamet plays Danny Vance in this drama that explores how the internet has changed the way teenagers and parents communicate. The film follows a group of high schoolers and their families as they navigate the complexities of identity and connection in the digital age.

2. “Worst Friends” (2014)

In this indie comedy, Chalamet plays the younger version of Sam. The story follows two childhood friends whose relationship is put to the test after one of them is injured in an accident and needs help during recovery.

1. “Burning Blue” (2014)

Chalamet plays Dylan Lynch in this romantic drama set within the U.S. Navy. The film follows two fighter pilots who fall in love during a government investigation into a series of accidents, forcing them to hide their relationship under the “Don’t Ask, Don’t Tell” policy.

Timothée Chalamet movies FAQ

Where can I watch "Marty Supreme"?

"Marty Supreme" is currently available on digital VOD platforms like Prime Video and Apple TV as of Feb. 10, 2026. It is scheduled to make its streaming debut on Max in April 2026.

How many Oscar nominations does Timothée Chalamet have?

As of 2026, Timothée Chalamet has three Academy Award nominations for Best Actor. These include "Call Me by Your Name" (2017), "A Complete Unknown" (2024), and "Marty Supreme" (2025).

Is there a third "Dune" movie coming out?

Yes! "Dune: Part Three" (adapting "Dune Messiah") wrapped production in Nov. 2025 and is currently scheduled for a theatrical release on Dec. 18, 2026.

What to binge next

  • ‘Marty Supreme’: Trailer and Streaming Info
  • 2026 Oscar Predictions and Frontrunners
  • How To Watch the Oscars Without Cable

The post How Many Timothée Chalamet Movies Can You Watch Before the 2026 Oscars? appeared first on CableTV.com.

item.title: FCC Proposes Lifeline Program Changes, Could Affect Low-Income Internet Access
item.dc:creator: Eric Chiu
item.description:
The FCC has proposed changes to the Lifeline internet subsidy program that could affect low-income internet access.

The post FCC Proposes Lifeline Program Changes, Could Affect Low-Income Internet Access appeared first on CableTV.com.
item.content:encoded:
Is the FCC changing Lifeline?

Last month, the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) voted to consider adding additional eligibility restrictions to its Lifeline internet subsidy program. The Lifeline program, which provides a $9.25 monthly internet and telephone credit to qualifying low-income households, could potentially see some of the following changes:

  • Formally restricting Lifeline to U.S. citizens.
  • Requiring a 5-year waiting period for qualifying non-citizens or those going through the immigration approval process.
  • Requiring applicants to enter their full Social Security number rather than their last 4 digits.
  • Changing eligibility requirements to a “1-per-address” rule that may affect households with multiple families.

The FCC argues that these changes are designed to root out financial waste from the Lifeline program. In its news release, the FCC cited a January internal investigation that found the program lost $10.5 million from 2020 to 2025 due to funds being credited to deceased individuals or issued twice.

That said, this total charge is less than than 1% of Lifeline’s annual budget, which was allotted at $2.9 billion for 2026 alone. Similarly, administrative improvements were only a fraction of the FCC’s proposed Lifeline changes, which focused heavily on citizenship verification. While these proposed updates have a long way to go before they potentially become policy, industry analysts argue that the FCC’s changes would create additional roadblocks for families who need low-income internet.

How proposed Lifeline changes affect low-income internet

Lifeline is already a relatively underused program for several reasons. The FCC-affiliated Universal Service Administration Company estimates that only 21% of qualified applicants enrolled in Lifeline during 2025.

Similarly, Lifeline’s base $9.95 monthly credit doesn’t come close to covering standard internet plans from most providers, which start at around $50 per month. Instead, most Lifeline customers have to settle for specialty low-income internet plans with sub-100 Mbps speeds that make gaming or high-definition streaming difficult.

Multiple industry groups blasted the FCC’s vote as a way to make it harder for legal immigrants and low-income households to get online.

“Everyone deserves affordable phone and internet service,” said Jenna Leventoff, Senior Policy Counsel for the American Civil Liberties Union, in a statement. “We are concerned that FCC proposals to limit Lifeline eligibility, increase data collection about program users, and to phase out phone-only service will further widen the digital divide. We urge the FCC to reconsider these harmful proposals so that everyone can stay connected.”

All of these factors suggest that the biggest effect of the FCC’s proposed changes will be to make it even harder for low-income families to sign up for Lifeline and get internet access. We’ll point out that Lifeline already requires applicants to be enrolled in a qualifying government assistance program or show that their earnings are below the federal poverty line.

In an era when the digital divide is more pronounced than ever, these proposed policy changes appear to benefit ISPs far more than working households.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we have years of experience covering the biggest internet and telecom news. Stay tuned with us to learn more about the biggest TV and tech stories and see how they affect you. For more about us, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news

  • ‘Doctor Who’ Star Dazzles In An Iconic Play—Stream Free For A Limited Time
  • Disney Just Renewed 4 Shows, but Canceled an Award-Winning Favorite
  • Soccer Viewership Is Exploding Ahead of the 2026 World Cup: Is Your TV Setup Ready?

The post FCC Proposes Lifeline Program Changes, Could Affect Low-Income Internet Access appeared first on CableTV.com.

item.title: The Latest Nintendo Direct Finally Revealed Yoshi’s Surprising Voice Actor
item.dc:creator: Olivia Bono
item.description:
The final "Super Mario Galaxy Movie" cast is here! Meet Yoshi's actor and watch the new trailer with our Nintendo Direct recap.

The post The Latest Nintendo Direct Finally Revealed Yoshi’s Surprising Voice Actor appeared first on CableTV.com.
item.content:encoded:
Happy Mar10 Day! (sorta)

Nintendo celebrated Mar10 Day early this year by releasing a Nintendo Direct on March 9. It’s not really clear why the company chose not to save the announcement for the 10th, but the Direct was exciting enough to make up for the confusion.

That’s because, alongside a new trailer (which you can watch below), we also got new casting announcements for a few more characters—including the long-anticipated Yoshi.

Watch the entire Nintendo Direct

Nintendo Directs are usually livestreams announcing new titles for Nintendo consoles, like the Switch and Switch 2. But sometimes they’re about other Nintendo products—like the horrifying talking flower from Super Mario Bros. Wonder. And ever since Nintendo partnered up with Illumination, they’ve also been about casting announcements.

If you’re serious about avoiding Nintendo Direct spoilers, you can watch the full eight-minute presentation, featuring cameos from the voice cast, below. But if you’d rather just skip to the good stuff, I don’t blame you! Read on to find out the new casting announcements.

New “Super Mario Galaxy Movie” cast members

The last cast members Nintendo revealed were Benny Safdie as Bowser Jr. and Brie Larson as Princess Rosalina. Both actors appeared in this week’s Direct, and it was heartwarming to see Brie Larson talk about watching Nintendo Directs and playing Nintendo games herself.

This time around, Illumination’s CEO Chris Meledandri announced three new cast members. Let’s find out who they are together!

Wart: Luis Guzmán

You might recognize Luis Guzmán from his recent appearance as Gomez Addams in Netflix’s “Wednesday.” Come this April, you’ll be able to hear his voice as Wart, the classic Mario villain who first appeared in “Yume Kōjō: Doki Doki Panic.” Yume Kōjō was later re-themed into a Mario game, bringing Wart into the Mario mythos.

Honey Queen: Issa Rae

In “The Super Mario Galaxy Movie,” Honey Queen rules over the Honeyhive Galaxy, which is the bee-themed location from the first “Super Mario Galaxy” game. She’ll be voiced by Issa Rae, the comedian who created and starred in the HBO comedy series “Insecure” in 2016. But kids will probably be familiar with her as President Barbie or Spider-Woman.

Yoshi: Donald Glover

And now for the main event. Fans have been speculating about who would eventually voice Yoshi since we first saw his egg appear in “The Super Mario Movie”‘s end credits scene. But I’m not sure that anyone could have predicted Childish Gambino himself, Donald Glover, would answer the call.

Glover actually retired the Childish Gambino persona in 2024, although part of me wonders if Yoshi will be doing some sort of musical collab with Jack Black’s Bowser. After all, the first movie’s song “Peaches” was nominated for Best Original Song at the 2024 Golden Globes, and the studio would probably love to reproduce its success. I could be off the mark, though. Donald Glover got his start in comedy—he might just do a killer Yoshi impression.

How to watch “The Super Mario Galaxy Movie”

“The Super Mario Galaxy Movie” is coming to theaters starting April 1—and no, that’s not an April Fool’s joke. It’s the sequel to “The Super Mario Movie,” which, as of writing, is currently streaming on Peacock.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, our entertainment experts are fans first. Whether it’s in the theater or in front of a TV, we’re always looking out for the next big thing. So if you need up-to-date entertainment news, you can trust our team to be there with the scoop. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • “Zootopia 2” Comes to Streaming
  • Best New Kids Movies on Streaming
  • Stream Cartoon Network Titles for Free

The post The Latest Nintendo Direct Finally Revealed Yoshi’s Surprising Voice Actor appeared first on CableTV.com.
